<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> She would sometimes say, "Lord, we thank you for our preacher Richard.<|5.20|>
<|5.20|> Your spirit has anointed him.<|7.74|>
<|7.74|> Help him keep going."<|11.04|>
<|11.04|> She would pray for people on the streets of Memphis who were looking that day for food<|17.48|>
<|17.48|> and for shelter.<|19.86|>
<|19.86|> She herself had lived on those streets.<|25.90|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> The amazing thing about Jesus's parable is not what it tells us about ourselves, but<|14.82|>
<|14.82|> what it tells us about Almighty God.<|18.10|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|1.74|> God really listens to us.<|7.74|>
<|8.84|> God really wants to hear us speak in our own words.<|17.84|>
<|19.20|> God doesn't always answer our prayers as quickly as we would like.<|24.46|>
<|24.46|> The boyfriend might not come by 5:00 p.m.<|27.90|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> the healing might not come exactly.<|6.10|>
<|6.10|> And we know as Episcopalians,<|8.76|>
<|8.76|> we should never confuse our exact petitions with what God ultimately will deliver.<|17.74|>
<|17.74|> That may be in the words of scripture more than we could ever ask or imagine.<|26.82|>
